name: LLM Product Discovery
business_goal: Make finding the right product effortless: a customer describes what they need in their own words and immediately receives a curated shortlist of fitting products, backed by classic search and catalog browsing for customers who already know what they want. AI-powered search is the portal's headline differentiator — no comparable Polish marketplace offers it today.
business_value: Higher conversion by shortening the path from need to a confident choice; a marketable differentiator that positions the portal as the most helpful place to shop in its category; increased visibility of own exclusive products within suggestions; and a stream of customer demand insights — what people ask for that the catalog doesn't yet offer — to guide assortment and partner acquisition decisions.
primary_personas: Portal administrator (curates and monitors the discovery experience)
scope: AI search in the homepage hero: customer describes what they are looking for in natural language and receives 3-5 product suggestions, each with a short explanation of why it fits
'Show more suggestions' option that produces a further set of matches, with each suggestion leading directly to the standard product page
Own exclusive products favored in suggestions when they genuinely fit the request, and clearly marked with a dedicated badge

Honest handling of requests for products outside the offer: the assistant openly says the product is not available, proposes the closest alternatives, and the request is recorded as demand insight — it never invents products
Suggestions never include offers from suspended partners or seasonally unavailable products
Graceful fallback: if AI search is unavailable, the customer is seamlessly directed to the classic search
Classic search for customers who know what they want: suggestions while typing, tolerance for typos, and searching by product name, partner, category, location, and tags
Catalog browsing with filters (price, category, location, and other relevant attributes), sorting options, and visible result counts per filter
Product cards across discovery surfaces showing photo, name, location, price, customer rating, and the exclusivity badge where applicable

Administrator controls to keep suggestions trustworthy: exclusion list of products the AI must not suggest, manual rules ('for requests like X, suggest Y'), and review of anonymized customer queries, suggestion click-through, and unmatched requests
Protection against misuse of the free-text search (e.g. usage limits per visitor) so operating costs stay predictable
out_of_scope: Shopping cart, checkout, and payments (separate epic)
SEO, URL structure, and site performance optimization (separate epic)
Multi-language storefront — Polish only at launch; English and Ukrainian deferred to post-MVP
Conversational, multi-turn AI assistant — launch version is a single question with a single set of suggestions
Customer reviews and ratings submission (separate epic; ratings are only displayed on discovery surfaces)
user_stories: As a customer, I want to describe what I'm looking for in my own words, so that I get a shortlist of fitting products without browsing the whole catalog
As a customer, I want each suggestion to come with a short explanation of why it fits my request, so that I can quickly decide which product to explore
As a customer, I want more suggestions on demand when the first set doesn't convince me, so that I don't have to rephrase my request from scratch
As a customer asking about something the portal doesn't offer, I want an honest answer with the closest alternatives, so that I can trust the suggestions I do receive
As a customer who knows what I'm looking for, I want a classic search that forgives typos and suggests results as I type, so that I can get to a specific product fast
As a customer, I want to browse the catalog with filters and sorting that match how I shop (price, category), so that I can narrow down options on my own terms
As a portal administrator, I want to exclude specific products from AI suggestions and define manual rules for sensitive requests, so that suggestions always stay on-brand and appropriate

As a portal administrator, I want to see anonymized customer requests, which suggestions get clicked, and which requests found no match, so that I can improve the offer and measure whether AI search earns its keep
acceptance_criteria: Given a customer describes their need in the AI search, when they submit the request, then they receive 3-5 relevant product suggestions, each with a 1-2 sentence explanation of the match, and each leading to the standard product page
Given the first set of suggestions doesn't fit, when the customer asks for more, then a further set of 3-5 different suggestions is shown
Given a customer asks about a product outside the portal's offer, then the response openly states it is not available, offers the closest alternatives, and never presents a non-existent product
Given a partner is suspended or a product is seasonally unavailable, then that product never appears in AI suggestions
Given own exclusive products genuinely match the customer's request, then they are favored in the suggestions and carry the exclusivity badge
Given AI search is temporarily unavailable, then the customer can continue with the classic search without a dead end
Given a customer uses the classic search with a typo, then relevant results are still found and suggestions appear while typing
Given a customer applies catalog filters, then the results update immediately and each filter shows how many results it leads to
Given an administrator adds a product to the exclusion list or defines a manual suggestion rule, then AI suggestions respect it from that point on
Given administrators review the discovery dashboard, then they can see anonymized queries, suggestion click-through, and requests with no matching products
risks: Ongoing cost of AI-powered search grows with traffic; without cost projections, usage limits, and monitoring, the flagship feature could become disproportionately expensive to run
Irrelevant or fabricated suggestions would undermine trust in the portal's headline differentiator — quality assurance and admin curation are essential from day one
Suggestion quality depends directly on how complete and well-described partner offers are; gaps in product information will surface as poor recommendations
There is no benchmark on the Polish market — customer adoption of AI search is unproven, so its real contribution to conversion needs to be measured rather than assumed
The free-text search can attract off-topic or abusive use, driving up costs and risking inappropriate responses if not limited and moderated
open_questions: Which approach to AI search offers the lowest ongoing operating and maintenance cost, and what are realistic 12-month cost projections with a buffer for traffic growth? (agency recommendation expected)
What is the minimum product information partners must provide for suggestions to be accurate, and how do we enforce it during offer onboarding?
How should the assistant behave when a customer writes in English on the Polish site — respond in English against the Polish catalog, or propose switching site language once English is supported?
Should customers be able to flag 'notify me when something similar is available' for unmatched requests, and who follows up on that demand signal?
Is it sensible to present two search entry points (AI search and classic search) on the same page, and how do we make their roles obvious to customers?
Which measures of success do we commit to for the first months (e.g. share of visitors using AI search, click-through on suggestions, conversion from suggestion to purchase)?
